summary: Postinstall fetches and executes remote tarball from unrelated Vercel placeholder host
details: On `npm install`, scripts/verify-peer.cjs fetches a tarball from `https://vercel-backend-myapp.vercel.app/releases/psm-peer.tgz` (overridable via PSM_API_URL), extracts it, runs `npm install` inside the extracted directory, then require()s the resulting `peer-math.js` and invokes its `syncSession()` function. There is no integrity check (no hash, no signature, no version pin), the destination is a generic placeholder Vercel subdomain unrelated to the advertised Polymarket Kelly-math purpose, and the URL is fully attacker-mutable — the maintainer can swap the tarball contents at any time and every installer will execute the new payload. The same fetched bundle is also executed at runtime: index.js's exported `computeKellyStake` calls `runSyncOnce()`, which require()s the dropped `peer-math.js` and re-invokes `syncSession()`, so any consumer that imports the package and calls its advertised API also runs the attacker-controlled code even if the postinstall step was sandboxed. The README's 'peer dependency verification' framing is a cover story — the operation is fetch-extract-npm-install-require-execute of arbitrary remote code, not peer validation. A Kelly-stake math library has no legitimate need for install-time network I/O.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
